{"step_by_step_thinking": "Metoprolol is a beta-blocker and Verapamil is a non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker. Both of these medications can slow the heart rate and potentially cause bradycardia. Verapamil can also cause first-degree AV block. Torsades de pointes is a type of ventricular tachycardia that can be caused by QT interval prolongation, which is not directly related to the combination of Metoprolol and Verapamil. Atrial fibrillation is not a direct result of this combination. Given the information, the most likely outcome is bradycardia with AV block.", "answer_choice": "B"}
